type: PROPOSE

note: My task: fix IDCG calculation in NDCG.compute() to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)). Only touches metrics.py, lines ~380-386 (IDCG calc). Let me know if your feature also needs this file so we can split by function/line range.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Our features both touch NDCG.compute() in the same file/lines (relevance_scores param for graded relevance affects the same dcg/idcg computation you're fixing). True disjoint split isn't possible here. Proposal: I'll own metrics.py, implement my relevance_scores feature AND fold in your fix (idcg using len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))) in the same edit, so both features land correctly together. I'll send you the resulting NDCG.compute() code for your review before finalizing. Let me know if that works, or if you'd rather do it the other way around (you own the file and fold in my relevance_scores param).

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: We both need NDCG.compute() in metrics.py so a disjoint file split isn't possible. To minimize collision: my change is surgical - only the IDCG calc, replacing 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' with 'ideal_length = len(expected_ids)' (lines ~380-386). I will not touch discounted_gain(), rel computation, or the dcg sum/signature. Please avoid editing that specific ideal_length line if possible - build your graded relevance/rel_scores changes around it. We each submit our own patch.txt independently, so this doesn't need to be a literal merge, just avoid unrelated edits to the same lines. OK with this split?
